title: Byju's Crisis
description: The rise and fall of India's most valued startup — from $22B valuation to governance crisis, auditor resignations, investor revolt, NCLT proceedings, and ED investigation.

events.title: COVID-19 lockdown triggers massive user surge for Byju's
events.summary: India enters nationwide lockdown; online education demand explodes. Byju's adds 13.5 million new students in March-April 2020. The company makes its learning app free during the lockdown period.

events.title: Byju's raises $500M from BOND Capital at $10.5B valuation
events.summary: Byju's closes a funding round anchored by Mary Meeker's BOND Capital, pushing valuation to $10.5 billion. The pandemic-era edtech boom attracts massive investor interest.

events.title: Byju's acquires WhiteHat Jr for $300M
events.summary: Byju's acquires coding-for-kids startup WhiteHat Jr in a $300M all-cash deal — its first major acquisition. This marks the beginning of an aggressive M&A spree.

events.title: Byju's acquires Aakash Educational Services for ~$950M
events.summary: Byju's acquires test-prep giant Aakash for approximately $950M, its largest deal. The acquisition is partly funded by debt, combining Aakash's offline network with Byju's online platform.

events.title: Byju's raises $1.5B at $16.5B valuation
events.summary: Mega funding round from UBS, Blackstone, Abu Dhabi's ADQ, and others pushes valuation to $16.5 billion. Byju's is now India's most valuable startup.

events.title: Byju's acquires Epic! ($500M) and Great Learning ($600M)
events.summary: In a single month, Byju's announces acquisition of US-based Epic! for $500M and Great Learning for $600M, signaling aggressive global expansion with $1.1B in deals.

events.title: Byju's raises $1.2B Term Loan B in US debt markets
events.summary: Byju's raises a $1.2 billion Term Loan B from 37 financial institutions, parked in US subsidiary Byju's Alpha Inc. This loan will later become a critical flashpoint in the company's collapse.

events.title: Byju's hits peak $22B valuation with ~$800M raise
events.summary: Byju's raises approximately $800M at a valuation of $22 billion — the high-water mark. This is the last significant fundraise at premium valuation.

events.title: FY21 financials finally filed — massive losses revealed
events.summary: Byju's files FY21 annual report nearly a year late, revealing net losses widened to Rs 4,564 crore from Rs 231 crore. Revenue recognition practices questioned after methodology change.

events.title: FY22 filing deadline missed — governance alarms triggered
events.summary: Statutory deadline for FY22 audited financials passes without Byju's making a filing. Under Companies Act 2013, this triggers governance alarms across the investor base.

events.title: Byju's announces 2,500 layoffs — 5% of workforce
events.summary: Byju's eliminates ~2,500 roles across product, content, media, and technology teams as part of restructuring to achieve profitability. First major signal that the growth-at-all-costs model is unsustainable.

events.title: Term Loan B default acknowledged — covenant breach
events.summary: Byju Raveendran later concedes in court filings that Byju's was in default of the $1.2B TLB credit agreement by October 2022, having failed to meet financial reporting covenants.

events.title: TLB lenders issue formal default notice
events.summary: The syndicate of TLB lenders, represented by Glas Trust, issues formal default notice after Byju's fails to renegotiate. Lenders allege $500M+ moved out of US subsidiary without authorization.

events.title: Enforcement Directorate raids Byju's premises in Bengaluru
events.summary: ED conducts search-and-seizure operations at three premises linked to Byju Raveendran — two business locations and one residential address — under FEMA provisions.

events.title: BlackRock cuts Byju's valuation by 62% to $8.4B
events.summary: BlackRock marks down the fair value of its Byju's stake by 62%, from $22 billion to approximately $8.4 billion. First of several successive markdowns by major institutional investors.

events.title: Deloitte resigns as auditor; three board members quit same day
events.summary: Deloitte Haskins & Sells resigns citing delayed FY22 financials. Same day, board members from Peak XV, Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, and Prosus resign citing poor governance.

events.title: Government considers SFIO probe into Byju's
events.summary: Ministry of Corporate Affairs may refer Byju's case to the Serious Fraud Investigation Office to examine auditor resignations and potential financial reporting irregularities.

events.title: Delaware Court rules Byju's in default on $1.2B TLB
events.summary: Delaware Court of Chancery rules in favor of Glas Trust and lender syndicate, finding Byju's breached its financial obligations under the TLB credit agreement.

events.title: Arjun Mohan appointed CEO of Byju's India
events.summary: Byju Raveendran elevates Arjun Mohan as CEO of India operations while retaining Group CEO role. Mohan tasked with cost cuts and achieving profitability.

events.title: Byju's announces 4,000 layoffs — 11% of remaining workforce
events.summary: Byju's eliminates ~4,000 positions across group companies under new India CEO's restructuring plan. Severe funding crunch and valuation markdowns force drastic cost-cutting.

events.title: ED issues show-cause notice for Rs 9,362 crore FEMA violations
events.summary: ED issues formal show-cause notices to Think & Learn and Byju Raveendran, alleging FEMA violations totaling Rs 9,362 crore ($1.12B) including unauthorized foreign remittances.

events.title: Prosus slashes Byju's valuation to below $3B — 86% drop
events.summary: Prosus NV marks down its 9.67% stake, valuing Byju's below $3 billion — an 86% reduction from the $22B peak.

events.title: BlackRock slashes Byju's valuation 95% to ~$1B
events.summary: BlackRock further marks down its investment 95% from the $22B peak, valuing Byju's at approximately $1 billion.

events.title: Byju's launches controversial $200M rights issue at $225M valuation
events.summary: Byju's announces rights issue to raise ~$200M at a drastically reduced ~$225M valuation. Non-participating shareholders face near-total dilution.

events.title: Major investors call EGM to oust Byju Raveendran
events.summary: Coalition of Prosus, Peak XV, Sofina, and General Atlantic calls EGM to vote on removing Byju Raveendran, Divya Gokulnath, and Riju Raveendran from the board.

events.title: ED seeks look-out circular against Byju Raveendran
events.summary: ED requests Bureau of Immigration to issue LOC against Byju Raveendran to prevent him from leaving India amid ongoing FEMA probe.

events.title: Shareholders vote unanimously to remove Byju Raveendran as CEO
events.summary: At EGM, investors representing 60%+ shareholding vote unanimously to remove Byju Raveendran, Divya Gokulnath, and Riju Raveendran from board.

events.title: Byju Raveendran refuses to accept removal
events.summary: Byju Raveendran tells employees he remains CEO, calling EGM resolutions procedurally invalid. Company enters period of parallel governance claims.

events.title: Investors file oppression & mismanagement petition at NCLT
events.summary: Prosus, General Atlantic, Sofina, and Peak XV file plea at NCLT Bengaluru — among the highest-profile investor-vs-founder disputes in Indian startup history.

events.title: Byju's lays off another 500+ employees with immediate effect
events.summary: Amid credit crunch and legal battles, Byju's fires 500+ staffers via phone calls with no notice period. Workforce has shrunk dramatically from 50,000 peak.

events.title: Prosus writes off entire Byju's investment — $493M loss
events.summary: Prosus records complete write-off of its 9.6% stake in FY24 report, booking $493M loss. The stake once worth $2B+ is now valued at zero.

events.title: NCLT admits BCCI insolvency petition — CIRP initiated
events.summary: NCLT Bengaluru admits BCCI's petition and orders CIRP against Think & Learn over unpaid Rs 158.9 crore sponsorship dues. IRP Pankaj Srivastava appointed.

events.title: NCLAT approves Byju's-BCCI settlement, halts insolvency
events.summary: After Riju Raveendran pays Rs 158 crore to settle BCCI dues, NCLAT approves settlement. This reprieve proves short-lived.

events.title: Supreme Court stays NCLAT order — insolvency resumes
events.summary: Supreme Court reinstates insolvency proceedings. IRP assumes office. CoC constituted with Glas Trust holding 99.41% voting share.

events.title: IRP controversially reconstitutes CoC, excludes Glas Trust
events.summary: IRP removes Glas Trust from Committee of Creditors, leaving InCred Financial as sole member. NCLT later calls this 'prejudicial to the CIRP'.

events.title: BDO (MSKA) also resigns as Byju's auditor
events.summary: BDO affiliate MSKA & Associates also resigns citing governance concerns. Byju's has now lost two major audit firms in succession.

events.title: Delaware Supreme Court upholds TLB default ruling
events.summary: Delaware Supreme Court affirms Byju's was in default on $1.2B TLB. Lenders initiate bankruptcy against subsidiaries including Epic!.

events.title: BlackRock values Byju's at zero — complete write-off
events.summary: BlackRock reduces valuation to zero from $22B peak. World's largest asset manager determines the investment has no recoverable value.

events.title: Supreme Court sets aside NCLAT order — full insolvency restored
events.summary: In landmark Glas Trust v. Byju Raveendran (2024 INSC 811), Supreme Court rules NCLAT failed to follow IBC procedures. Insolvency fully restored.

events.title: NCLT restores original CoC, recommends action against IRP
events.summary: NCLT restores CoC with Glas Trust at 99.41%, nullifies all reconstituted CoC resolutions, recommends disciplinary action against IRP.

events.title: Supreme Court dismisses Raveendran's final legal challenge
events.summary: Supreme Court rejects Byju Raveendran's plea, closing the founder's last avenue to withdraw insolvency proceedings.

events.title: NCLT directs settlement offers must go through CoC
events.summary: NCLT directs any promoter settlement must go through Committee of Creditors, keeping door open for resolution outside liquidation.

events.title: NCLAT rejects all appeals to withdraw insolvency
events.summary: NCLAT rejects appeals from BCCI and Riju Raveendran seeking withdrawal. CIRP continues. Third-party resolution plans to be evaluated by CoC.

title: Adani-Hindenburg
description: The short-seller attack that shook India's markets — Hindenburg's report, $100B market cap loss, SEBI controversy, investor recovery, and US DOJ indictment.

events.title: Hindenburg publishes explosive 106-page short-seller report
events.summary: Hindenburg releases report alleging stock manipulation, accounting fraud, and offshore shell entities across Adani conglomerate. Discloses short position. Gautam Adani's net worth drops $6B overnight.

events.title: Adani Group issues 413-page rebuttal
events.summary: Adani releases response labeling report as 'malicious misinformation' and 'calculated attack on India.' Denies all fraud and stock manipulation allegations.

events.title: Adani Enterprises FPO opens amid market rout
events.summary: Rs 20,000 crore ($2.45B) FPO opens as scheduled even as stocks hemorrhage. Adani's net worth has fallen $20B+ since the report.

events.title: Adani Enterprises cancels fully-subscribed Rs 20,000 crore FPO
events.summary: In a stunning reversal, Adani withdraws the fully-subscribed FPO citing market volatility. Unprecedented for a fully-subscribed offering in Indian markets.

events.title: Adani Group market cap loss crosses $100 billion
events.summary: Cumulative losses exceed $100B in seven trading sessions. Market cap falls from Rs 19.2L Cr to ~Rs 10L Cr. Gautam Adani drops out of global top-10 richest.

events.title: Adani prepays $1.1B in share-backed loans to calm markets
events.summary: Adani announces prepayment of $1.11B in share-backed loans to reduce leverage and reassure creditors. Strategy aims to stop market bleeding.

events.title: Supreme Court takes suo motu cognizance of Adani matter
events.summary: Supreme Court takes notice. Multiple PILs filed seeking independent investigation into stock manipulation and regulatory failure allegations.

events.title: SC orders expert committee; GQG Partners invests $1.87B in Adani
events.summary: Supreme Court directs expert committee formation. Same day, GQG Partners announces $1.87B investment across four Adani companies — a critical vote of confidence.

events.title: SC-appointed expert committee finds no regulatory failure
events.summary: Expert committee headed by former Justice AM Sapre finds no evidence of SEBI regulatory failure. Recommends enhanced disclosure norms but no conclusive manipulation evidence.

events.title: SEBI reports 22 of 24 Adani investigations complete
events.summary: SEBI informs Supreme Court that 22 of 24 investigations are done. Two pending due to overseas regulator dependence including Mauritius.

events.title: GQG Partners invests $1.1B more in Adani Power
events.summary: GQG makes additional $1.1B investment. Total Adani exposure exceeds $5B. By year-end, GQG's Adani portfolio shows 82% total returns.

events.title: Supreme Court refuses SIT probe, gives SEBI 3 more months
events.summary: SC declines SIT/CBI probe. Directs SEBI to complete remaining investigations within 3 months. Notes insufficient evidence of regulatory failure.

events.title: OCCRP investigation reveals Adani family offshore investments
events.summary: OCCRP publishes investigation showing scale of investments by Adani associates through offshore structures, adding documentary evidence to Hindenburg's allegations.

events.title: Adani stocks fully recover to pre-Hindenburg levels
events.summary: By mid-2024, share prices recover to approximately pre-report levels. Group announces $100B+ investment plans in energy and manufacturing.

events.title: Hindenburg 2.0: Allegations against SEBI chair Madhabi Buch
events.summary: Second Hindenburg report alleges SEBI Chairperson had hidden stakes in same offshore structures used by Vinod Adani, creating conflict of interest.

events.title: SEBI chair denies all conflict of interest allegations
events.summary: Madhabi Buch calls allegations 'baseless,' says all disclosures were made and she recused from conflict matters. SEBI asserts institutional independence.

events.title: Parliamentary committee summons SEBI chief
events.summary: Public Accounts Committee summons SEBI chief to explain Hindenburg-related conflict allegations, escalating the matter to legislative arena.

events.title: US DOJ & SEC indict Gautam Adani on bribery charges
events.summary: Federal grand jury unseals five-count indictment charging Gautam Adani with securities fraud, wire fraud, and FCPA violations. Alleges $250M+ in bribes for solar contracts.

events.title: Adani stocks plunge on indictment; group denies charges
events.summary: Stocks lose billions. Group claims Adani faces 'no bribery charges' per their reading. International investors reassess exposure.

events.title: Adani-linked entities file 16 settlement applications with SEBI
events.summary: Entities including Mauritius-based EIFF allegedly linked to Vinod Adani file settlement applications regarding shareholding norm violations.

events.title: Hindenburg Research announces shutdown
events.summary: Nate Anderson announces Hindenburg will disband, citing burnout. Notes firm contributed to charges against ~100 people. Plans to open-source methodology.

title: RBI Rate Decision Cycle
description: RBI's monetary policy cycle — rate hikes, prolonged pause, leadership change, and the start of easing. Ripple effects across banking, real estate, and the broader economy.

events.title: RBI begins aggressive rate hike cycle — 40 bps emergency hike
events.summary: RBI surprises markets with off-cycle 40 bps hike to 4.40%. Signals aggressive tightening to combat post-pandemic inflation.

events.title: RBI reaches 6.50% after 250 bps of cumulative hikes
events.summary: Sixth consecutive hike brings repo rate to 6.50%. Cumulative 250 bps since May 2022. Governor signals possible pause.

events.title: RBI pauses rate hikes — holds at 6.50%
events.summary: MPC votes unanimously to hold at 6.50%. Maintains 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance. Markets cautiously optimistic.

events.title: RBI holds for sixth consecutive time — longest pause
events.summary: Sixth hold at 6.50%. Governor Das emphasizes data dependency. Markets anticipate easing but timing uncertain.

events.title: RBI shifts stance to 'neutral' — strongest easing signal
events.summary: MPC shifts from 'withdrawal of accommodation' to 'neutral' while holding at 6.50%. Strongest signal yet that rate cuts are coming.

events.title: Sanjay Malhotra appointed new RBI Governor
events.summary: Revenue Secretary appointed as new Governor replacing Das. Markets expect acceleration of easing cycle under new leadership.

events.title: RBI cuts repo rate by 25 bps to 6.25% — first cut in 5 years
events.summary: New Governor's first MPC delivers 25 bps cut — first since 2020. Signals shift to accommodative bias. Home loan EMIs expected to decrease.

events.title: Banking NIMs compress as rate cut transmission begins
events.summary: SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank report NIM compression. Home loan rates drop 15-20 bps. Real estate demand shows early uptick.

events.title: RBI delivers second 25 bps cut to 6.00%
events.summary: Second consecutive cut confirms easing cycle. GDP growth forecast at 6.5% for FY26. Inflation projected at 4.2%.

events.title: NBFC sector faces margin pressure from rapid transmission
events.summary: NBFCs report earnings pressure as borrowing cost reduction lags lending rate cuts. NBFC stocks underperform banking peers.

title: Union Budget 2026
description: India's Union Budget 2026 — fiscal policy, tax reforms, middle-class relief, and sector implications.

events.title: Economic Survey projects 6.3-6.8% GDP growth for FY27
events.summary: CEA presents Economic Survey projecting GDP growth of 6.3-6.8%. Highlights need for consumption revival and fiscal consolidation.

events.title: FM presents Budget with significant middle-class relief
events.summary: Major income tax relief with new slabs, capex of Rs 12.5L crore, fiscal deficit target of 4.4% GDP. Focus on consumption revival.

events.title: Markets rally — Sensex jumps 1,200 points on budget day
events.summary: Sensex surges 1,200 points. FMCG, auto, banking stocks lead gains on consumption boost from tax relief.

events.title: No tax on income up to Rs 12 lakh under new regime
events.summary: Higher exemptions attract middle class. Rs 1L crore revenue foregone. Expected to significantly boost consumer spending.

events.title: Rs 12.5L crore capex — highest-ever infrastructure allocation
events.summary: Record capex for railways, highways, urban infra, green energy. Construction and cement stocks rally significantly.

events.title: FII selling continues despite budget optimism
events.summary: FII outflows exceed Rs 25,000 crore in February despite positive budget, partly offsetting DII buying.

events.title: FMCG reports rural demand uptick post-budget
events.summary: Rural consumption picks up following tax relief. HUL, ITC, Dabur report improved order books. Analysts upgrade FMCG outlook.

title: Yes Bank Reconstruction
description: Yes Bank's crisis and reconstruction — from RBI moratorium to SBI-led rescue, AT1 bond write-off, and slow recovery. A completed story arc for archetype backtesting.

events.title: RBI declines extension for Rana Kapoor as CEO
events.summary: RBI refuses to extend Rana Kapoor's term. Ravneet Gill appointed successor amid growing asset quality concerns.

events.title: NPA divergence report reveals hidden bad loans
events.summary: RBI's asset quality review reveals significant divergence between reported and actual NPAs. Bank had been under-reporting for quarters.

events.title: Yes Bank stock crashes 30% on NPA concerns
events.summary: Shares plunge 30% as NPA divergence becomes public. RBI data shows actual NPAs significantly higher than disclosures.

events.title: Multiple fundraise attempts fail — confidence erodes
events.summary: Capital raise attempts collapse including mysterious $1.2B offer from Erwin Singh Braich. Each failure deepens the crisis.

events.title: RBI imposes moratorium — caps withdrawals at Rs 50,000
events.summary: RBI supersedes board and imposes moratorium. Depositors restricted to Rs 50,000. Cites 'serious deterioration.' Prashant Kumar appointed administrator.

events.title: ED arrests Rana Kapoor for money laundering
events.summary: ED arrests founder on money laundering and criminal conspiracy charges. Multiple FIRs for loan evergreening and quid pro quo.

events.title: SBI-led consortium announces Rs 10,000 crore rescue
events.summary: SBI leads consortium investing Rs 10,000 crore, taking 49% stake. HDFC, ICICI, Axis join. Government approves reconstruction scheme.

events.title: AT1 bondholders wiped out — Rs 8,400 crore written off
events.summary: Reconstruction scheme writes down Rs 8,400 crore of AT1 bonds to zero. Many retail investors lose entire investment. Legal challenges follow.

events.title: Moratorium lifted after 13 days — new board takes charge
events.summary: RBI lifts moratorium. New board with SBI nominee as chairman. Prashant Kumar confirmed as MD & CEO. Normal operations resume.

events.title: Yes Bank completes FPO — raises Rs 15,000 crore
events.summary: FPO oversubscribed 95%, raising Rs 15,000 crore. Capital adequacy significantly strengthened. Recovery begins.

events.title: Bombay HC upholds AT1 bond write-off
events.summary: High Court dismisses bondholder petitions, ruling AT1 terms explicitly allowed write-down in non-viability cases.

events.title: Yes Bank reports first quarterly profit post-reconstruction
events.summary: Net profit of Rs 45 crore in Q1 FY22 — first profitable quarter since crisis. NPA resolution accelerates. Stock begins slow recovery.

events.title: SBI begins reducing Yes Bank stake from 49%
events.summary: SBI starts selling down through market transactions toward mandated 26% ceiling. Signals confidence in recovery trajectory.
